{{< pagebreak >}}

# Day 7 — Build your personal awareness plan {#day7}

```{=typst}
#wcg-lead[Today you gather the week into one simple plan that belongs to you.]
#wcg-lesson-art("plan", caption: [Seven small habits combining into one steady plan])
```

## Why this matters

Insights fade without a little structure. A when-then plan helps carry an
intention into action,^9^ and pulling your best ideas onto one page makes them
easy to return to.

There is also a deeper reason. As your confidence grows, you are building what
psychologists call self-efficacy — linked with how much effort people invest and
how long they persist.^13^ This week was never really about one coffee or one
cart. It was about that quiet, steady confidence.

## An everyday example

A finished plan might read: "I'm watching my evening phone-shopping. When the urge
shows up, I pause and add items to a list. I moved the app off my home screen. On
hard days, I'll reread this page." Simple, personal, repeatable.

```{=typst}
#wcg-action[
  Complete your one-page awareness plan. Keep it somewhere you will see it.
]
```

> **Notice.**
>
> Which one habit from this week is most worth keeping?

This plan is a living thing. Change it whenever your life changes — that is using
it well, not failing to follow it.

{{< include assets/worksheets/ws-7-awareness-plan.qmd >}}
